ddls:
CREATE TABLE "emotion_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each emotion category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the emotion category (e.g., Neutral, Happy, Anger) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the emotion category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* Parent category ID for hierarchical categorization */,
  "category_code" TEXT /* Unique code for each emotion category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "emotion_classifications" (
  "classification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each classification result */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source data (e.g., survey, user interaction) */,
  "target_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the target emotion category being classified */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model used for emotion classification */,
  "classification_date" TEXT /* Date when the emotion classification was performed */,
  "neutral_score" REAL /* Score for the Neutral emotion */,
  "happy_score" REAL /* Score for the Happy emotion */,
  "anger_score" REAL /* Score for the Anger emotion */,
  "disgust_score" REAL /* Score for the Disgust emotion */,
  "fear_score" REAL /* Score for the Fear emotion */,
  "sad_score" REAL /* Score for the Sad emotion */,
  "surprise_score" REAL /* Score for the Surprise emotion */,
  "boredom_score" REAL /* Score for the Boredom emotion */,
  "confusion_score" REAL /* Score for the Confusion emotion */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("classification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_emotion_classifications_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_emotion_classifications_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_emotion_classifications_target_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("target_category_id") REFERENCES emotion_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of data source (e.g., survey, user interaction, social media) */,
  "source_description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "source_location" TEXT /* Location or context where the data was collected */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the data source (if applicable) */,
  "source_format" TEXT /* Format of the data source (e.g., CSV, JSON, text) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "model_version" TEXT /* Version of the model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of machine learning model (e.g., neural network, decision tree) */,
  "model_description" TEXT /* Description of the model */,
  "training_date" TEXT /* Date the model was trained */,
  "training_data" TEXT /* Information about the training data used for the model */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the model in percentage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., research, marketing) */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "classification_id" INTEGER /* ID of the classification result being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the result */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the result was accessed */,
  "access_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user accessed the result */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the user's access in seconds */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_classification_id FOREIGN KEY ("classification_id") REFERENCES emotion_classifications ("classification_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being evaluated */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the emotion category being evaluated */,
  "evaluation_date" TEXT /* Date when the model performance was evaluated */,
  "precision" REAL /* Precision of the model for the category */,
  "recall" REAL /* Recall of the model for the category */,
  "f1_score" REAL /* F1 score of the model for the category */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the model for the category */,
  "mean_squared_error" REAL /* Mean squared error of the model for the category */,
  "mean_absolute_error" REAL /* Mean absolute error of the model for the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_performance_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES emotion_categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_performance_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)
